summary: A corporate overview of The Wonderful Company detailing its history, divisions, and the foundational philosophy of its founders, Stewart and Lynda Resnick. The text highlights their commitment to healthy products, environmental stewardship, and corporate philanthropy.

principles.statement: Measure business success by community impact, not just financial returns.
principles.rationale: Acting as a good corporate citizen is a personal responsibility, and true success includes how much a company gives back to its communities and contributes to the world.
principles.source_quote: Instead of just gauging success by numbers on a spreadsheet, they measure it by how much they give back to their communities and contribute to the world.

principles.statement: The most nutritious and best-tasting foods come directly from nature.
principles.rationale: What people put into their bodies matters, which drives the company's focus on producing healthy, natural agricultural products.
principles.source_quote: As farmers, we believe that what you put into your body matters. The most nutritious and best-tasting foods are those that are from nature.

anti_patterns.description: Gauging business success solely by numbers on a spreadsheet.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It ignores a leader's personal responsibility to act as a good corporate citizen and give back to the community.
